What Was Done: Restaurant POS systems capture historical sales, transactions, and payments, but provide limited predictive support for short-term planning.
Why It Matters: Defines why forecasting is needed beyond static reporting.

What Was Done: Real POS data from Twisted Bar was collected and consolidated with external weather and event-related variables.
Why It Matters: Creates the empirical foundation for restaurant demand forecasting.

What Was Done: Dates, sales, transactions, refunds, missing values, and daily aggregation logic were standardized and validated.
Calendar, seasonality, weather, event, lag, rolling average, and KPI-based features were created.
Why It Matters: Transforms raw POS and weather data into predictive variables.

What Was Done: Baseline, SARIMAX, Random Forest, Hybrid SARIMAX + RF, and KPI-based models were evaluated.
Why It Matters: Allows comparison between benchmark, statistical, machine learning, and hybrid approaches.

What Was Done: Performance improved from baseline to SARIMAX and then to the Hybrid SARIMAX + RF approach.
Why It Matters: Shows what improved, why it improved, and how much it improved.

What Was Done: Forecast outputs were translated into staffing, menu preparation, and promotion planning recommendations.
Conformal prediction is planned to add lower and upper demand intervals around point forecasts.
Why It Matters: Adds uncertainty-aware forecasting for better operational planning.
